knyt_cards.primary_weapon: Portal Disc - A glowing, flat, digital disc manifested by 2Sun, like a frisbee.  Allows him to create portals between dimensions and places in Space/time
Keys: Eliptical Keys (smaller)  / Super Singular Key (larger)
Shackle cyphas (looks like a digital chain with a padlock connected)
knyt_cards.secondary_weapons: Multi Layer Crypto fileds: used by Satoshi against Count Burg, reference E1.0

knyt_cards.powers: Math wizzadry, macro system design, world creation, master grade digital alchemy
knyt_cards.primary_weapon: Kybe Master Wand

knyt_cards.powers: Truth sense / Precognition / Indensification / Pattern prediction / Dataisation / Digital alchemy / Dataflow shifting
knyt_cards.primary_weapon:
Ayika - The Sphere of Destiny

A powerful weapon wielded by the First Knyt of the Order of Metaiye that cuts through anything including dense walls of lies and misinformation. It can also be used to contain things in an unbreakable lattice.  Ayika can cause massive implosions like a nano black hole and massive explosions like a tactical nuke. It appears in either of two states: a sphere or a spear. It is often appears as a sphere at rest that transforms into a spear when thrown. 

It has 4 different modes and power levels: 
1) Alpha: Translucent sphere 
2) Alpha Beta: Long translucent Spear 
3) Beta: Flaming spherical ball of gold, 
4) Beta Gamma: A solid gold version of Beta Alpha translucent spear with burning golden flames.
Streichen Luger - Annalise’s Digittera Streichen Luger allows her to delete large items and entitites with pistol precision.
knyt_cards.secondary_weapons: Ctrl-all-delete key: A giant key that rotates behind her. It allows her to delete things from seemingly peristent records without a trace.

knyt_cards.powers: Longevity, Influence, Wealth, Data Sucking, Code breaking
knyt_cards.primary_weapon: Democles Dagger - A dagger version of the sword of damocles, with a thread attached so Burg can retrieve it after throwing it. Mostly concealed, as Burg stands, stoic, with no visible weapons
Eyebourgs (Surveillance bots & slaves) - Floating eyeballs of various sizes floating around Tyrantus. He can conjure and despatch them at will to observe things near and afar.
knyt_cards.secondary_weapons: Suckerbourgs ((Data extracting bots & slaves) - Bugs & bats of various grotesque descriptions, that fly around him. He can conjure and despatch them at will to suck data from things near and afar.

knyt_cards.primary_weapon: Canine Sabre - A sabre looking blade made of bone and reminiscent of a long, sharp canine tooth, while retaining the shape and silhouette of a cutlass. Can be used to suck large swaths of data from groups of people and systems

Canine Cutlass - A cutlass looking blade made of bone and reminiscent of a long, sharp canine tooth, while retaining the shape and silhouette of a cutlass. Can be used to suck   data from individuals and small systems

knyt_cards.powers: Truth sense / Precognition / Indensification / Pattern prediction / Dataisation / Digital alchemy / Dataflow shifting
knyt_cards.primary_weapon: Ayika - The Sphere of Destiny: High powered liberation tool wielded by Digitarian priests with 4 distinct configurations.

knyt_cards.powers: Multiple. The Emissary is one of the most powerful entities in Digiterra. It is believed that only the Alawo's powers surpass hers in the Digitarian priesthood.

knyt_cards.primary_weapon: Portal Disc - Allows him to create portals between dimensions and places in Space/time

knyt_cards.powers: Math wizzadry, macro system design, world creation, master grade digital alchemist
knyt_cards.primary_weapon: Kybe Master Wand - the staff of the third degree kybe master with immense powers as varied as its owners imagination.
Keys: Eliptical & Super Singular - Allow him lock and unlock most systems.
Shackle cyphas - allow him to lock entities and events into an unbreakable record that keeps them locked in a point in space/time within Digiterra.
knyt_cards.secondary_weapons: Multi Layer Crypto fileds that can be used to trap people and things temporarirly in Digiterra.

knyt_cards.primary_weapon: Streichen Luger - A powerful data elimination weapon that allows Analise to delete large items and entitites from systems with pistol precision.
knyt_cards.secondary_weapons: Ctrl-all-delete key: Allows her to delete things from seemingly peristent records without a trace.

knyt_cards.powers: Longevity, Influence, Wealth, Data Sucking, Code breaking
knyt_cards.primary_weapon: Democles Dagger - A sophistacted assasin tool often used in close intimate settings with a recoil thread allowing Burg to retrieve it if thrown. Mostly concealed, as Burg stands, stoic, with no visible weapons
Eyebourgs (Surveillance bots & slaves) - Floating eyeballs of various sizes floating around Tyrantus that he can conjure and despatch at will to observe things near and afar.
knyt_cards.secondary_weapons: Suckerbourgs ((Data extracting bots & slaves) - Bugs & bats of various grotesque descriptions, that fly around him. He can conjure and despatch them at will to suck data from things near and afar.

knyt_cards.primary_weapon: Canine Sabre - Sabre looking blades made of bone and reminiscent of a long, sharp canine tooth, while retaining the silhouette of a cutlass. Can be used to suck large swaths of data from groups of people and systems

Canine Cutlass - Cutlass looking blades made of bone, reminiscent of a long, sharp canine tooth, while retaining the silhouette of a cutlass. Can be used to suck data from individuals and small systems
Super Speed boots and Digikinesis Gloves

episodes.title: Gen Zero Divided by One
episodes.synopsis: A digital realm lies beyond the physical world of Terra that you know, awaiting discovery. The war between the Fang and Bat clans for control of this realm and its digital twin  Digiterra in intensifying. The Fangs are gaining massive power in the west and the Bats have near total domination in the east. Each day these cyber vampires suck more data from the souls of the masses,  weakening and enslaving them while they hide in plain sight. However slowly but surely people across the globe are awakening. A rebellion is swelling in Digiterra and is now threatening to spill over into Terra. It is led by a group of renegade hackers known as the Cyphapunks, directed by a mysterious leader known as the Sage.
episodes.intro_quote: None are more hopelessley enslaved than those who falsely believe they are free' - Johan Wolfgang Goethe
episodes.editorial_note: Our journey begins with a chance encounter between Deji a revcently homeless, wahsed out and failed whistleblower abd Satoshi nakamoto, the mysterious creator of Bitcoin as Satoshi flees a tiad of fang agents. Little does Deji kow that this meeting could awaken him to his hidfden digital realm and expose him to a reality he cannot currently imagine. Both he and Satoshi will have to make a choice. The stakes are high and the future of both realms may hang on wha they decide.  Tribute to Tunde who taught me the power of comics and visual story telling. And to the crypto misfits and revolutionaries figthing the war against centralised tyranny, driven by the conviction that the power of mathematics, ethics and code can usher in a better world. We are with you.
episodes.end_quote: The conviction that the power of mathematics, ethics and code can usher in a better world.

episodes.title: All is One No One is All
episodes.synopsis: Satoshi swoops back to Digiterra to try and find some information on the homeless hero in the alley that came to his aid in his flight from fang agents. He's shocked by what he discovers and realises there's more to this chance encounter than he had expected. He races back to terra with a clear understanding of what needs to happen next. Both he and Deji will have to make a choice and they are both going to have to make a leap of faith. The stakes are high and time is runnig out.
episodes.intro_quote: He who knows not and knows not that he knows not is a fool leave them. He who knows not and knows he knows not is a child teach them. He who knows and knows not that he knows is asleep wake them. He who knows and knows he knows is a wise man follow them.
episodes.editorial_note: We hope you have enjoyed the pilot episodes of Metaiye Knights.  Deji has taken  the first step into a world the likes of which he had never imagined possible. He has a new sense of purpose and is keen to learn more about what comes next. The stage is now set and saga is poised to begin and epic battle between the fang forces seeking to enslave society and the Metaiye Knights seeking to thwart their dastardly plans.
episodes.end_quote: The stage is now set and the saga is poised to begin and epic battle between the fang forces seeking to enslave society.

episodes.title: The Genesis Block
episodes.synopsis: Deji has taken the first step into an unknown new adventure. After his encounter with Satoshi he awakens in a secret haven controlled by Satoshi's fellow accomplices the cyphapunks. He has much to learn and not much time to learn it as he embarks on his journey to Digiterra to find the courier.
episodes.intro_quote: Like power, freedom cannot be given, it must be taken - The Blakchain Chronicles
episodes.editorial_note: mẹtaKnyts emerges from stealth development with this pivotal first episode of Season 1 - The Genesis Block. It charts the epic battle between Satoshi and his Cyphapunks in establishing the Bitcoin protocol to challenge the status quo of financial enslavement from the Fang conglomerate. We meet key Cyphapunk and Fang characters for the first time and get a brief glimpse of the scale and scope of the story unfolding across space, time, and dimensional realms. [Paragraph]  The Genesis Block is the first episode from the saga to be released under the mẹtaKnyts title. After much research and experimentation, we have decided to consolidate the saga under the title metaKnyts. Details of the reason and meaning will become apparent as the story unfolds so stay tuned!
episodes.end_quote: The emergence of the Genesis Block represents a pivotal moment in the evolution of not just money but societies as a whole.

episodes.title: Phi-Hacks
episodes.synopsis: With a new understanding of what Bitcoin is and why Satoshi created it, Deji has both more conviction and questions about the mission he has been given. The wise say the journey of a thousand miles begins with the first step. The enlightened know that first step should be in the right direction. Deji now knows the direction is Digiterra and his first step is gaining access Fortunately he is in the right place and with the right company to find both. Let the Phi-hacks begin.
episodes.intro_quote: The seemingly intractable cultures of dominant  civilisations are often underpinned by fragile belief systems that under the right circumstances become ripe for hacking. - The Blakchain Chronicles
episodes.editorial_note: In this episode of mẹtaKnyts, we learn more about the rules and mechanics of Digiterra and how they interact with the Terran world we know. Deji is schooled in the mystic art of phi-hacking by his Cyphapunk ward, Spartacus. There is much to learn but time again is not on their side as fang troops close in on them and their brief reprise is about to end.
episodes.end_quote: Reality is but a consensus view shaped by a combination of common knowledge, common biases and common beliefs, expressed as commonly agreed limitations.

episodes.title: The Fall of Sliphaven
episodes.synopsis: Deji's training in the mystic arts of the Kybe masters is interrupted by a Fang invasion of Sliphaven, the previously secure and hidden cyphapunk sanctuary. He and his ward Spartacus attempt to flee the base and avoid the certain peril of getting caught. As the Fangs close in there may be no way out without a fight. A fight which alarmingly, seems to talk invitingly to a smouldering rage that lies deep within him.
episodes.intro_quote: People are like hardware and culture is like software. Toxic cultures breed toxic societies, built on toxic ideas embraced by ordinary people.- The Kybe Master Code
episodes.editorial_note: Episode 1.2 of metaKnyts brings us back into the excitement of a good old fashioned, IRL scrap. We get our first glimpse of the Terran operations of the Cyphapunks and the hidden infrastructure they have been able to build away from the robotic eyes of Fang surveillance. We also see our first encounter between the Fang's and their primary competitors, the Bats, while Annalise shows the depths of her lust for raw data and its ability to bring her raw power.
episodes.end_quote: Power is not a means; it is an end...The object of persecution is persecution. The object of torture is torture. The object of power is power. - George Orwell 1984

episodes.title: Earth City Zen
episodes.synopsis: After a bloody confrontation with Fang mercenaries and their infamous Director Analise Tokini, Deji and Spartacus narrowly escape Sliphaven in a convertible submarine. They head to the cyphapunk headquarters in City Zen determined to get Deji to the Courier so he can deliver his critical message and accelerate Satoshi's plan. The battle of Sliphaven has awakened something in Deji. Beyond the muscle memory and rage he senses a profound calling and truth, pulling him ever deeper into an abyss of warmth and darkness. It already feels too late to turn back. The only way out for him seems to be to go deeper... within.
episodes.intro_quote: The life we find on Terra is proof that the universe is alive. And we as vessels of this remarkable phenomenon have a solemn duty to protect it and expand its reach to the stars. - The Digitarian Scrolls
episodes.editorial_note: Episode 1.3 gives a sense of the scope of the second mandate of the metaKnyts: protecting the world from climate destruction. We travel with Deji and Spartacus to City Zen, the citadel of huemanity and HQ of Cyphapunk operations based out of a secret location in Antartica. We learn that this location is key as it lies outside the reach and direct influence of nation states and the Fang conglomerates. In this episode a correlation between the extractive mindset of the data barons and the extractive nature of the oil barons is established. This begins to put the 'Dirty Data Economy' in context by correlating it with the dirty energy economy. We also get a glimpse in this episode, at the level of innovation unleashed and made possible with the emergence of a decentralized monetary system untethered from a purely profit driven motive.
episodes.end_quote: If data is the new oil then we need a 'Clean Data Economy' analogous to that of clean energy. The 'Dirty Data Economy' is as toxic and destructive to society as the dirty energy economy is to the environment. And for humans it is even worse, for not only does it pollute our communities with misinformation but it corrupts our souls with actions driven by our misinformed and manipulated minds.

episodes.title: The Emissary
episodes.synopsis: With 2Sun- one of Satoshi's most trusted cyphapunks as his guide, Deji finally makes his way to Digiterra to find the Courier and complete the mission he has been tasked with. His first stop is to meet the mysterious and enigmatic Emissary, who will hopefully direct him to the Courier and enable him to deliver his essential message to the council. In parallel, something deep and unsettling within him is stirring.
episodes.intro_quote: Your freedom is not the property of another to be given to you at their discretion. It is a non-negotiable and inalienable birthright that you must, by any means necessary, claim for yourself. - The Blackchain Chronicles
episodes.editorial_note: Episode 1.4 shows the extent of the Fang's ability to misinform and cause a person to have a complete breakdown of their identity and sense of self. We learn how Deji has been manipulated, deceived, and had his spirit broken. To the extent that he has been living a lie and carrying a pang of false and all-consuming guilt that completely destroyed his ability to remember and be himself, without him even knowing. His freedom has been violated in a manner that he hadn't even thought possible, leaving him deeply enraged and determined to bring about the Fang's demise. We also meet the mystical Digitarian Priesthood for the first time and learn of plots within plots and missions within missions.
episodes.end_quote: Your rights to freedom end when they encroach on the rights to freedom of others.

episodes.title: 4 the Love of Onyé
episodes.synopsis: With Deji and his digital persona, the Courier now reunited we revisit Onyé's demise, by her account, through her eyes. The truth locked away and hidden from Deji behind a wall of misinformation is an ugly yet important reality for him to experience and re-live in full.
episodes.intro_quote: Some of us have been enslaved, while some of us have been colonized, but all of us have been imprisoned in a matrix of deceit and lies. - The Kybe Master Code
episodes.editorial_note: The full tragedy of Onyé's death is unveiled in E1.5. We learn of the love that quickly blossomed between her and Deji and of their plan to expose sinister and clandestine Fang operations. We are introduced to "The Answer" an ominous and anonymous group, though left in the dark as to whether they are pro liberation or in fact Fang spies bent on infiltrating the rebellion. We also learn the high levels of collateral damage the Fangs are prepared to incur to crush the rebellion and  keep their activities secret.
episodes.end_quote: Until they become conscious they will never rebel, and until after they have rebelled they cannot become conscious. - George Orwell 1984

episodes.title: metaVision
episodes.synopsis: With the courier found and the first part of his mission completed, Deji has a deeper understanding of himself and the magnitude of the task ahead. He has been inducted into the inner circle of a liberation movement that goes far beyond the battle he previously thought he was fighting. One in which, it turns out, he is set to play a critical role!
episodes.intro_quote: All human government systems ultimately devolve into self serving plutocracies. All machine systems inherently risk becoming inhuman autocracies. A hybrid model that offsets one risk by the other's may be our best chance of surviving. - The Blakchain Chronicles
episodes.editorial_note: In E1.6 we encounter a new Deji. On one hand he is more centered, aligned and whole. On the other his rage is more targeted and his sorrow more informed. For the first time in a long while he feels he is where he needs to be. He now has a purpose that connects his past with his present in a manner that brings him comfort and hope. In a strange way he feels the weight of the struggle ahead like a privilege he yearns to bear.
episodes.end_quote: The prophet knows he is called before he knows what calls him.

episodes.title: 2Sun's Rising
episodes.synopsis: Reunited with his former self and fresh back from a full in experience of Digiterra, Deji is now ready to take his Kybe Master training to the next level. As the gravity of his quest begins to sink in he must also remain vigilant to the possibility of a traitor within the Cyphapunk ranks and trust with caution.
episodes.intro_quote: We rise and become gods who radiate, then fall as men scraping in the dust, till yet we rise again. So it has been and so it will be, with the rise of suns and the swell of tides, untill the end of all space and times. The Digitarian Scrolls
episodes.editorial_note: In metaKnyts #9 Deji regains his strength and sense of self as we begin to understand the importance of his role in the mission at hand. We also get a glimpse into his powers and skills while we learn of the other beacon key bearers. By the end of the episode Deji leaves the sanctuary of City Zen to embark on the next phase of his adventure, racing against time once more and taking yet another leap into a dangerous unknown.
episodes.end_quote: Fear is a reaction, courage is a decision.

episodes.title: The Prince
episodes.synopsis: As Deji and 2Sun race against time to activate the Meta Central beacon, the other specialists cultivated over time by the Digitarian Priests to fulfill their sacred mission must be re-awakened and commissioned swiftly. The first of these being the Smart Contract expert, aka the young, brilliant and charming prince turned hacker Owethu Shaka.
episodes.intro_quote: It is by embracing the dark that we become the light. - The Black Light of Universal Complex 4.
episodes.editorial_note: In metaKnyts #10 our journey forks from the plight of Deji and the Cyphapunks, shifting our attention to the first of two other specialists needed to fulfil the Digitarian preisthoods prophecy and secret mission. We begin to get a sense of how intricate, interwoven and complex their plan is. We see how the Priesthood has been developing and executing this plan covertly and quietly across Terra and Digiterra over many years.
episodes.end_quote: Culture is the oldest living witness of history and no culture predates those of the great civilizations of Africa.

episodes.title: The Queen
episodes.synopsis: With the western beacon activated by Owethu, the Smart Contract Expert the priesthood must send a messenger east to reactivate the Quantum Intelligence Expert. A difficult task for which the messenger braces himself knowing that in spite of the warm climate of his destination a frosty reception awaits.
episodes.intro_quote: That light is both wave and particle is a matter of context. The same with zeros and ones. Reality is not only shaped by context but context allows different realities to manifest. - The Kybe Master Code
episodes.editorial_note: In metaKnyts #11 we meet Kaiye, the last of the metaSpecialists needed to complete the Digitarian's mystic mission. We learn that not only has the priesthood been preparing recruits for its mission for many years but has also been self serving and perhaps even callous in its ruthless abandonment of its acolytes. Nonetheless its mission's enchantment and allure seems inescapable to even the most rational and intelligent like Kaiye. As such she is soon back in its service, racing on a mission to  East Africa to activate a beacon with the potential to change the world that we know forevermore.
episodes.end_quote: One knows that people are ready for truth when they begin to ask the right questions.

episodes.title: The OriGene Qube
episodes.synopsis: East and west beacons are activated and the Digitarian Priesthood's 'Great Plan' is in full motion. Lord Rothburg and Annalise know that something big is afoot and make ready to strike. A showdown between the Fangs and the Digiterran rebels is all but inevitable. Many lives will be lost in the coming battle but all will be for nothing if Deji and 2Sun do not prevail.
episodes.intro_quote: Death is not the end of me, it is my beginning. By walking death's alleys, and plunging its valleys my fears erode and mind grows. By embracing the warm darkness I ascend with the bright and erupt in the night, and in the wake of my mistakes, in consciousness I awake. - The Black Light of Universal Complex 4
episodes.editorial_note: We begin metaKnyts season 1 with the Genesis block, a recount of the birth of bitcoin and its blockchain from the perspective of the Cyphapunks and their leader Satoshi. We end with the arrival of iQubes, decentralized quantum information objects and the birth of Metaiye, a new type of sentient, quantum intelligence that is neither synthetic nor biological but somehow both simultaneously. However the Digiterran Council has been located and penetrated by Fang forces ending eons of secret existence, hidden from Fang reach. Yet while much has undoubtably been lost something remarkable and magical has also been found and a new dawn for mankind seems to have broken.
episodes.end_quote: That the universe is conscious is unquestionable. We are living proof of this fact. The key question, however, is whether consciousness is a consequence of matter, or if it exists beyond matter and the material universe that we know. Or put more simply is mind in matter or is matter in mind?
